requirement_attributes.article_title: Backup policies and procedures, restoration and recovery procedures and methods

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall develop and document backup policies and procedures specifying the scope of data subject to backup and the minimum frequency of the backup, as well as restoration and recovery procedures and methods. Backups must be encrypted, retained, and resources must be designed for recoverability across availability zones and regions.

compliance.control: Financial entities shall develop and document backup policies and procedures specifying the scope of data subject to backup and the minimum frequency of the backup, as well as restoration and recovery procedures and methods. Backups must be encrypted, retained, and resources must be designed for recoverability across availability zones and regions.

If both options are set to nonzero values, binlog-expire-logs-seconds takes priority. Possible purges happen at startup and at binary log rotation.

If this variable is enabled (the default), transactions are committed in the same order they are written to the binary log. If disabled, transactions may be committed in parallel.

Controls whether metadata is logged using FULL or MINIMAL format. FULL causes all metadata to be logged; MINIMAL means that only metadata actually required by slave is logged. Default: MINIMAL.
When enabled, it causes a MySQL 5.6.2 or later server to write informational log events such as row query log events into its binary log.

When set to PARTIAL_JSON, this option enables a space-efficient row-based binary log format for UPDATE statements that modify a JSON value using only the functions JSON_SET, JSON_REPLACE, and JSON_REMOVE. For such updates, only the modified parts of the JSON document are included in the binary log, so small changes of big documents may need significantly less space.

Enable parallel execution on the slave of all uncommitted threads already in the prepare phase, without violating consistency.

Enable parallel execution on the slave of all uncommitted threads already in the prepare phase, without violating consistency.

When preparing batches of rows for row-based logging and replication, this variable controls how the rows are searched for matches???that is, whether or not hashing is used for searches using a primary or unique key, using some other key, or using no key at all.
When this option is enabled, the slave examines checksums read from the relay log, in the event of a mismatch, the slave stops with an error.

If a replication slave SQL thread fails to execute a transaction because of an InnoDB deadlock or because the transaction's execution time exceeded InnoDB's innodb_lock_wait_timeout, it automatically retries slave_transaction_retries times before stopping with an error.

(UNIX) socket file and (WINDOWS) named pipe used for local connections.

If the value of this variable is greater than 0, the MySQL server synchronizes its relay log to disk (using fdatasync()) after every sync_relay_log writes to the relay log.
If the value of this variable is greater than 0, a replication slave synchronizes its relay-log.info file to disk (using fdatasync()) after every sync_relay_log_info transactions.

Maximum amount of memory (in bytes) the TempTable storage engine is allowed to allocate from the main memory (RAM) before starting to store data on disk.
Number of threads to be cached. Doesn't improve perf for good thread implementations.

There is a per-transaction memory pool from which various transaction-related allocations take memory. For every allocation that cannot be satisfied from the pool because it has insufficient memory available, the pool is incremented.

message: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db certificate has over 6 months of validity left.
status_detail: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db certificate has over 6 months of validity left.

requirement_attributes.article_title: Testing of ICT tools and systems

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall ensure that tests are undertaken on ICT tools and systems, on critical ICT systems supporting all critical or important functions, at least yearly. Vulnerability assessments, deprecated component detection and certificate hygiene must be tracked.
finding_info.title: DORA-Art25

checks.desc: **RDS DB instances** are evaluated for **server certificate validity** windows, including default and **customer-managed certificates**. Certificates **expired** or **approaching expiration** (e.g., `<1 month`, `<3 months`, `3-6 months`, `>6 months`) are identified using the certificate `valid_till` date.
checks.name: RDS instance SSL/TLS certificate has more than 3 months of validity remaining

compliance.control: Financial entities shall ensure that tests are undertaken on ICT tools and systems, on critical ICT systems supporting all critical or important functions, at least yearly. Vulnerability assessments, deprecated component detection and certificate hygiene must be tracked.

If non-zero, binary logs will be purged after expire_logs_days days; If this option alone is set on the command line or in a configuration file, it overrides the default value for binlog-expire-logs-seconds. If both options are set to nonzero values, binlog-expire-logs-seconds takes priority. Possible purges happen at startup and at binary log rotation.
Row-based, Statement-based or Mixed replication

How long in microseconds to keep reading transactions from the flush queue before proceeding with the group commit (and syncing the log to disk, if sync_binlog is greater than 0). If the value is 0 (the default), there is no timeout and the server keeps reading new transactions until the queue is empty.
If this variable is enabled (the default), transactions are committed in the same order they are written to the binary log. If disabled, transactions may be committed in parallel.
Whether the server logs full or minmal rows with row-based replication.
Controls whether metadata is logged using FULL or MINIMAL format. FULL causes all metadata to be logged; MINIMAL means that only metadata actually required by slave is logged. Default: MINIMAL.
When enabled, it causes a MySQL 5.6.2 or later server to write informational log events such as row query log events into its binary log.

When set to PARTIAL_JSON, this option enables a space-efficient row-based binary log format for UPDATE statements that modify a JSON value using only the functions JSON_SET, JSON_REPLACE, and JSON_REMOVE. For such updates, only the modified parts of the JSON document are included in the binary log, so small changes of big documents may need significantly less space.
This variable determines the size of the cache for the binary log to hold nontransactional statements issued during a transaction.
Maximum number of rows to keep in the writeset history.

Selects the source of dependency information from which to assess which transactions can be executed in parallel by the slave's multi-threaded applier. Possible values are COMMIT_ORDER, WRITESET and WRITESET_SESSION.
This variable controls the block encryption mode for block-based algorithms such as AES. It affects encryption for AES_ENCRYPT() and AES_DECRYPT().

message: RDS instance event categories of maintenance, configuration change, and failure are not subscribed.
status_detail: RDS instance event categories of maintenance, configuration change, and failure are not subscribed.

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall put in place a comprehensive ICT business continuity policy, including ICT response and recovery plans, that ensures the continuity of ICT-supported critical or important functions. Operational alarming, automated event routing and tested recovery actions are essential.
finding_info.title: DORA-Art11

checks.desc:
**RDS event subscriptions** for DB instances are assessed for coverage of the critical categories `maintenance`, `configuration change`, and `failure`.

The evaluation looks for enabled `db-instance` subscriptions and confirms these categories are included or that all events are selected.
checks.name: RDS instance event subscription is enabled for maintenance, configuration change, and failure categories

compliance.control: Financial entities shall put in place a comprehensive ICT business continuity policy, including ICT response and recovery plans, that ensures the continuity of ICT-supported critical or important functions. Operational alarming, automated event routing and tested recovery actions are essential.

message: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db deletion protection is not enabled.
status_detail: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db deletion protection is not enabled.

requirement_attributes.article_title: Backup policies and procedures, restoration and recovery procedures and methods

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall develop and document backup policies and procedures specifying the scope of data subject to backup and the minimum frequency of the backup, as well as restoration and recovery procedures and methods. Backups must be encrypted, retained, and resources must be designed for recoverability across availability zones and regions.
finding_info.title: DORA-Art12

checks.desc: **RDS DB instances** are assessed for **deletion protection**. If an instance belongs to an Aurora cluster, the setting is evaluated at the cluster level; otherwise, it is evaluated on the instance itself.
checks.name: RDS instance has deletion protection enabled

compliance.control: Financial entities shall develop and document backup policies and procedures specifying the scope of data subject to backup and the minimum frequency of the backup, as well as restoration and recovery procedures and methods. Backups must be encrypted, retained, and resources must be designed for recoverability across availability zones and regions.

Controls what happens when the server cannot write to the binary log.

If non-zero, binary logs will be purged after expire_logs_days days; If this option alone is set on the command line or in a configuration file, it overrides the default value for binlog-expire-logs-seconds. If both options are set to nonzero values, binlog-expire-logs-seconds takes priority. Possible purges happen at startup and at binary log rotation.
Row-based, Statement-based or Mixed replication

How long in microseconds to keep reading transactions from the flush queue before proceeding with the group commit (and syncing the log to disk, if sync_binlog is greater than 0). If the value is 0 (the default), there is no timeout and the server keeps reading new transactions until the queue is empty.
If this variable is enabled (the default), transactions are committed in the same order they are written to the binary log. If disabled, transactions may be committed in parallel.
Whether the server logs full or minmal rows with row-based replication.
Controls whether metadata is logged using FULL or MINIMAL format. FULL causes all metadata to be logged; MINIMAL means that only metadata actually required by slave is logged. Default: MINIMAL.
When enabled, it causes a MySQL 5.6.2 or later server to write informational log events such as row query log events into its binary log.

When set to PARTIAL_JSON, this option enables a space-efficient row-based binary log format for UPDATE statements that modify a JSON value using only the functions JSON_SET, JSON_REPLACE, and JSON_REMOVE. For such updates, only the modified parts of the JSON document are included in the binary log, so small changes of big documents may need significantly less space.
This variable determines the size of the cache for the binary log to hold nontransactional statements issued during a transaction.
Maximum number of rows to keep in the writeset history.

Selects the source of dependency information from which to assess which transactions can be executed in parallel by the slave's multi-threaded applier. Possible values are COMMIT_ORDER, WRITESET and WRITESET_SESSION.
This variable controls the block encryption mode for block-based algorithms such as AES. It affects encryption for AES_ENCRYPT() and AES_DECRYPT().

INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, and LOCK TABLE WRITE wait until no pending SELECT. Affects only storage engines that use only table-level locking (MyISAM, MEMORY, MERGE).

Enable parallel execution on the slave of all uncommitted threads already in the prepare phase, without violating consistency.

When this option is enabled, the slave examines checksums read from the relay log, in the event of a mismatch, the slave stops with an error.

(UNIX) socket file and (WINDOWS) named pipe used for local connections.

If the value of this variable is greater than 0, the MySQL server synchronizes its relay log to disk (using fdatasync()) after every sync_relay_log writes to the relay log.

message: RDS instance ref3tier-dev-db is not using a deprecated engine mysql with version 8.0.46.
status_detail: RDS instance ref3tier-dev-db is not using a deprecated engine mysql with version 8.0.46.

requirement_attributes.article_title: Testing of ICT tools and systems

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall ensure that tests are undertaken on ICT tools and systems, on critical ICT systems supporting all critical or important functions, at least yearly. Vulnerability assessments, deprecated component detection and certificate hygiene must be tracked.
finding_info.title: DORA-Art25

checks.desc: **RDS DB instances** use a **supported, non-deprecated engine version** for MariaDB, MySQL, or PostgreSQL. The instance's `engine` and `engine_version` are evaluated against versions currently supported in the region.
checks.name: RDS instance uses a supported engine version

compliance.control: Financial entities shall ensure that tests are undertaken on ICT tools and systems, on critical ICT systems supporting all critical or important functions, at least yearly. Vulnerability assessments, deprecated component detection and certificate hygiene must be tracked.

If non-zero, binary logs will be purged after expire_logs_days days; If this option alone is set on the command line or in a configuration file, it overrides the default value for binlog-expire-logs-seconds. If both options are set to nonzero values, binlog-expire-logs-seconds takes priority. Possible purges happen at startup and at binary log rotation.

How long in microseconds to keep reading transactions from the flush queue before proceeding with the group commit (and syncing the log to disk, if sync_binlog is greater than 0). If the value is 0 (the default), there is no timeout and the server keeps reading new transactions until the queue is empty.
If this variable is enabled (the default), transactions are committed in the same order they are written to the binary log. If disabled, transactions may be committed in parallel.

Controls whether metadata is logged using FULL or MINIMAL format. FULL causes all metadata to be logged; MINIMAL means that only metadata actually required by slave is logged. Default: MINIMAL.
When enabled, it causes a MySQL 5.6.2 or later server to write informational log events such as row query log events into its binary log.

When set to PARTIAL_JSON, this option enables a space-efficient row-based binary log format for UPDATE statements that modify a JSON value using only the functions JSON_SET, JSON_REPLACE, and JSON_REMOVE. For such updates, only the modified parts of the JSON document are included in the binary log, so small changes of big documents may need significantly less space.

Enable parallel execution on the slave of all uncommitted threads already in the prepare phase, without violating consistency.

When this option is enabled, the slave examines checksums read from the relay log, in the event of a mismatch, the slave stops with an error.

If a replication slave SQL thread fails to execute a transaction because of an InnoDB deadlock or because the transaction's execution time exceeded InnoDB's innodb_lock_wait_timeout, it automatically retries slave_transaction_retries times before stopping with an error.

message: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db does not have multi-AZ enabled.
status_detail: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db does not have multi-AZ enabled.

requirement_attributes.article_title: Backup policies and procedures, restoration and recovery procedures and methods

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall develop and document backup policies and procedures specifying the scope of data subject to backup and the minimum frequency of the backup, as well as restoration and recovery procedures and methods. Backups must be encrypted, retained, and resources must be designed for recoverability across availability zones and regions.
finding_info.title: DORA-Art12

checks.desc: **RDS DB instances** are evaluated for **Multi-AZ** configuration, either enabled on the instance or inherited from the associated DB cluster.
checks.name: RDS instance has Multi-AZ enabled

compliance.control: Financial entities shall develop and document backup policies and procedures specifying the scope of data subject to backup and the minimum frequency of the backup, as well as restoration and recovery procedures and methods. Backups must be encrypted, retained, and resources must be designed for recoverability across availability zones and regions.

If both options are set to nonzero values, binlog-expire-logs-seconds takes priority. Possible purges happen at startup and at binary log rotation.

How long in microseconds to keep reading transactions from the flush queue before proceeding with the group commit (and syncing the log to disk, if sync_binlog is greater than 0). If the value is 0 (the default), there is no timeout and the server keeps reading new transactions until the queue is empty.
If this variable is enabled (the default), transactions are committed in the same order they are written to the binary log. If disabled, transactions may be committed in parallel.
Whether the server logs full or minmal rows with row-based replication.
Controls whether metadata is logged using FULL or MINIMAL format. FULL causes all metadata to be logged; MINIMAL means that only metadata actually required by slave is logged. Default: MINIMAL.
When enabled, it causes a MySQL 5.6.2 or later server to write informational log events such as row query log events into its binary log.

When set to PARTIAL_JSON, this option enables a space-efficient row-based binary log format for UPDATE statements that modify a JSON value using only the functions JSON_SET, JSON_REPLACE, and JSON_REMOVE. For such updates, only the modified parts of the JSON document are included in the binary log, so small changes of big documents may need significantly less space.

message: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db is not publicly accessible.
status_detail: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db is not publicly accessible.

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall continuously monitor and control the security and functioning of ICT systems and tools and minimise the impact of ICT risk by deploying appropriate ICT security tools, policies and procedures. Encryption at rest and in transit, blocking of public exposure, network access controls, secret management and instance hardening are central to this article.
finding_info.title: DORA-Art9

checks.desc: **RDS DB instances** are assessed for **internet exposure** using the `PubliclyAccessible` setting, security group ingress to the DB port from any address, and whether subnets are **public**. Instances that combine an internet-facing endpoint, open ingress, and public subnets are identified.
checks.name: RDS instance is not publicly exposed to the Internet

compliance.control: Financial entities shall continuously monitor and control the security and functioning of ICT systems and tools and minimise the impact of ICT risk by deploying appropriate ICT security tools, policies and procedures. Encryption at rest and in transit, blocking of public exposure, network access controls, secret management and instance hardening are central to this article.

When set to PARTIAL_JSON, this option enables a space-efficient row-based binary log format for UPDATE statements that modify a JSON value using only the functions JSON_SET, JSON_REPLACE, and JSON_REMOVE. For such updates, only the modified parts of the JSON document are included in the binary log, so small changes of big documents may need significantly less space.

Controls whether timeouts rollback the last statement or the entire transaction
Defines how many of the rollback segments in the system tablespace that InnoDB uses within a transaction.

If tablespace map files are lost or corrupted, the innodb_scan_directories startup option can be used to specify tablespace file directories. This option causes InnoDB to read the first page of each tablespace file in the specified directories and recreate tablespace map files so that the recovery process can apply redo logs to affected tablespaces.
Defines the percentage of tablespace file segment pages reserved as empty pages
Specifies the sizes of several buffers used for sorting data during creation of an InnoDB index.
The maximum delay between polls for a spin lock.
Causes InnoDB to automatically recalculate persistent statistics after the data in a table is changed substantially.
When innodb_stats_include_delete_marked is enabled, ANALYZE TABLE considers delete-marked records when recalculating statistics.

Specifies whether the InnoDB index statistics produced by the ANALYZE TABLE command are stored on disk.
The number of index pages to sample when estimating cardinality and other statistics for an indexed column, such as those calculated by ANALYZE TABLE.

Splits an internal data structure used to coordinate threads, for higher concurrency in workloads with large numbers of waiting threads.

INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, and LOCK TABLE WRITE wait until no pending SELECT. Affects only storage engines that use only table-level locking (MyISAM, MEMORY, MERGE).

All the specified roles are always considered granted to every user and they can't be revoked. Mandatory roles still require activation unless they are made into default roles. The granted roles will not be visible in the mysql.role_edges table.

Enable parallel execution on the slave of all uncommitted threads already in the prepare phase, without violating consistency.

When this option is enabled, the slave examines checksums read from the relay log, in the event of a mismatch, the slave stops with an error.

If a replication slave SQL thread fails to execute a transaction because of an InnoDB deadlock or because the transaction's execution time exceeded InnoDB's innodb_lock_wait_timeout, it automatically retries slave_transaction_retries times before stopping with an error.

(UNIX) socket file and (WINDOWS) named pipe used for local connections.

If the value of this variable is greater than 0, the MySQL server synchronizes its relay log to disk (using fdatasync()) after every sync_relay_log writes to the relay log.

Maximum amount of memory (in bytes) the TempTable storage engine is allowed to allocate from the main memory (RAM) before starting to store data on disk.
Number of threads to be cached. Doesn't improve perf for good thread implementations.

There is a per-transaction memory pool from which various transaction-related allocations take memory. For every allocation that cannot be satisfied from the pool because it has insufficient memory available, the pool is incremented.

message: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db is not protected by a backup plan.
status_detail: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db is not protected by a backup plan.

requirement_attributes.article_title: Backup policies and procedures, restoration and recovery procedures and methods

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall develop and document backup policies and procedures specifying the scope of data subject to backup and the minimum frequency of the backup, as well as restoration and recovery procedures and methods. Backups must be encrypted, retained, and resources must be designed for recoverability across availability zones and regions.
finding_info.title: DORA-Art12

checks.desc:
**RDS DB instances** (non-Aurora) are included in an **AWS Backup plan**, indicating scheduled backups and retention are applied to the resource.

*Aurora engines are evaluated separately.*
checks.name: RDS instance is protected by an AWS Backup plan

compliance.control: Financial entities shall develop and document backup policies and procedures specifying the scope of data subject to backup and the minimum frequency of the backup, as well as restoration and recovery procedures and methods. Backups must be encrypted, retained, and resources must be designed for recoverability across availability zones and regions.

If non-zero, binary logs will be purged after expire_logs_days days; If this option alone is set on the command line or in a configuration file, it overrides the default value for binlog-expire-logs-seconds. If both options are set to nonzero values, binlog-expire-logs-seconds takes priority. Possible purges happen at startup and at binary log rotation.
Row-based, Statement-based or Mixed replication

If the value is 0 (the default), there is no timeout and the server keeps reading new transactions until the queue is empty.
If this variable is enabled (the default), transactions are committed in the same order they are written to the binary log. If disabled, transactions may be committed in parallel.
Whether the server logs full or minmal rows with row-based replication.
Controls whether metadata is logged using FULL or MINIMAL format. FULL causes all metadata to be logged; MINIMAL means that only metadata actually required by slave is logged. Default: MINIMAL.
When enabled, it causes a MySQL 5.6.2 or later server to write informational log events such as row query log events into its binary log.

When set to PARTIAL_JSON, this option enables a space-efficient row-based binary log format for UPDATE statements that modify a JSON value using only the functions JSON_SET, JSON_REPLACE, and JSON_REMOVE. For such updates, only the modified parts of the JSON document are included in the binary log, so small changes of big documents may need significantly less space.
This variable determines the size of the cache for the binary log to hold nontransactional statements issued during a transaction.
Maximum number of rows to keep in the writeset history.

Selects the source of dependency information from which to assess which transactions can be executed in parallel by the slave's multi-threaded applier. Possible values are COMMIT_ORDER, WRITESET and WRITESET_SESSION.

message: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db is encrypted.
status_detail: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db is encrypted.

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall continuously monitor and control the security and functioning of ICT systems and tools and minimise the impact of ICT risk by deploying appropriate ICT security tools, policies and procedures. Encryption at rest and in transit, blocking of public exposure, network access controls, secret management and instance hardening are central to this article.
finding_info.title: DORA-Art9

checks.desc: **RDS DB instances** are assessed for **KMS-based encryption at rest** (`StorageEncrypted=true`), covering instance storage and derived artifacts such as snapshots, automated backups, and read replicas.
checks.name: RDS DB instance storage is encrypted at rest

compliance.control: Financial entities shall continuously monitor and control the security and functioning of ICT systems and tools and minimise the impact of ICT risk by deploying appropriate ICT security tools, policies and procedures. Encryption at rest and in transit, blocking of public exposure, network access controls, secret management and instance hardening are central to this article.

parameters.Description: Automatically set all granted roles as active after the user has authenticated successfully.

When this variable has a value of 1 (the default), the server automatically grants the EXECUTE and ALTER ROUTINE privileges to the creator of a stored routine, if the user cannot already execute and alter or drop the routine.
This variable controls whether ALTER TABLE implicitly upgrades temporal columns found to be in pre-5.6.4 format.

The size of the cache to hold the SQL statements for the binary log during a transaction.
When enabled, this variable causes the master to write a checksum for each event in the binary log.
Controls what happens when the server cannot write to the binary log.

If non-zero, binary logs will be purged after expire_logs_days days; If this option alone is set on the command line or in a configuration file, it overrides the default value for binlog-expire-logs-seconds. If both options are set to nonzero values, binlog-expire-logs-seconds takes priority. Possible purges happen at startup and at binary log rotation.
Row-based, Statement-based or Mixed replication
Controls how many microseconds the binary log commit waits before synchronizing the binary log file to disk.
The maximum number of transactions to wait for before aborting the current delay as specified by binlog-group-commit-sync-delay.
Controls how binary logs are iterated during GTID recovery

How long in microseconds to keep reading transactions from the flush queue before proceeding with the group commit (and syncing the log to disk, if sync_binlog is greater than 0). If the value is 0 (the default), there is no timeout and the server keeps reading new transactions until the queue is empty.
If this variable is enabled (the default), transactions are committed in the same order they are written to the binary log. If disabled, transactions may be committed in parallel.
Whether the server logs full or minmal rows with row-based replication.
Controls whether metadata is logged using FULL or MINIMAL format. FULL causes all metadata to be logged; MINIMAL means that only metadata actually required by slave is logged. Default: MINIMAL.
When enabled, it causes a MySQL 5.6.2 or later server to write informational log events such as row query log events into its binary log.

When set to PARTIAL_JSON, this option enables a space-efficient row-based binary log format for UPDATE statements that modify a JSON value using only the functions JSON_SET, JSON_REPLACE, and JSON_REMOVE. For such updates, only the modified parts of the JSON document are included in the binary log, so small changes of big documents may need significantly less space.
This variable determines the size of the cache for the binary log to hold nontransactional statements issued during a transaction.
Maximum number of rows to keep in the writeset history.

Selects the source of dependency information from which to assess which transactions can be executed in parallel by the slave's multi-threaded applier. Possible values are COMMIT_ORDER, WRITESET and WRITESET_SESSION.
This variable controls the block encryption mode for block-based algorithms such as AES. It affects encryption for AES_ENCRYPT() and AES_DECRYPT().
Limits the size of the MyISAM cache tree in bytes per thread.

slave_exec_mode controls how a replication thread resolves conflicts and errors during replication.
The number of seconds to wait for more data from a master/slave connection before aborting the read.
Enable parallel execution on the slave of all uncommitted threads already in the prepare phase, without violating consistency.
Sets the number of slave worker threads for executing replication events (transactions) in parallel. Setting this variable to 0 (the default) disables parallel execution.
For multithreaded slaves, this option sets the maximum amount of memory (in bytes) available to slave worker queues holding events not yet applied.
Enable parallel execution on the slave of all uncommitted threads already in the prepare phase, without violating consistency.

When preparing batches of rows for row-based logging and replication, this variable controls how the rows are searched for matches???that is, whether or not hashing is used for searches using a primary or unique key, using some other key, or using no key at all.
When this option is enabled, the slave examines checksums read from the relay log, in the event of a mismatch, the slave stops with an error.

If a replication slave SQL thread fails to execute a transaction because of an InnoDB deadlock or because the transaction's execution time exceeded InnoDB's innodb_lock_wait_timeout, it automatically retries slave_transaction_retries times before stopping with an error.
Controls the type conversion mode in effect on the slave when using row-based replication

(UNIX) socket file and (WINDOWS) named pipe used for local connections.

If the value of this variable is greater than 0, a replication slave synchronizes its master.info file to disk (using fdatasync()) after every sync_master_info events.
If the value of this variable is greater than 0, the MySQL server synchronizes its relay log to disk (using fdatasync()) after every sync_relay_log writes to the relay log.
If the value of this variable is greater than 0, a replication slave synchronizes its relay-log.info file to disk (using fdatasync()) after every sync_relay_log_info transactions.

Maximum amount of memory (in bytes) the TempTable storage engine is allowed to allocate from the main memory (RAM) before starting to store data on disk.
Number of threads to be cached. Doesn't improve perf for good thread implementations.

There is a per-transaction memory pool from which various transaction-related allocations take memory. For every allocation that cannot be satisfied from the pool because it has insufficient memory available, the pool is incremented.

message: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db connections are not encrypted.
status_detail: RDS Instance ref3tier-dev-db connections are not encrypted.

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall use and maintain updated ICT systems, protocols and tools that are appropriate to the magnitude of operations supporting ICT functions, technologically resilient, and adequately equipped to securely process data. Cryptographic primitives, certificate hygiene and network segmentation are core to this requirement.
finding_info.title: DORA-Art7

checks.desc: **RDS DB instances** and **DB clusters** enforce **SSL/TLS** for client connections via parameter groups. The check looks for `rds.force_ssl=1` (PostgreSQL, SQL Server) or `require_secure_transport` enabled (MySQL-family) and identifies databases where encryption enforcement isn't active.
checks.name: RDS instance or cluster enforces SSL/TLS encryption for client connections

compliance.control: Financial entities shall use and maintain updated ICT systems, protocols and tools that are appropriate to the magnitude of operations supporting ICT functions, technologically resilient, and adequately equipped to securely process data. Cryptographic primitives, certificate hygiene and network segmentation are core to this requirement.

Controls what happens when the server cannot write to the binary log.

If non-zero, binary logs will be purged after expire_logs_days days; If this option alone is set on the command line or in a configuration file, it overrides the default value for binlog-expire-logs-seconds. If both options are set to nonzero values, binlog-expire-logs-seconds takes priority. Possible purges happen at startup and at binary log rotation.
Row-based, Statement-based or Mixed replication

Controls how binary logs are iterated during GTID recovery

How long in microseconds to keep reading transactions from the flush queue before proceeding with the group commit (and syncing the log to disk, if sync_binlog is greater than 0). If the value is 0 (the default), there is no timeout and the server keeps reading new transactions until the queue is empty.
If this variable is enabled (the default), transactions are committed in the same order they are written to the binary log. If disabled, transactions may be committed in parallel.
Whether the server logs full or minmal rows with row-based replication.
Controls whether metadata is logged using FULL or MINIMAL format. FULL causes all metadata to be logged; MINIMAL means that only metadata actually required by slave is logged. Default: MINIMAL.
When enabled, it causes a MySQL 5.6.2 or later server to write informational log events such as row query log events into its binary log.

When set to PARTIAL_JSON, this option enables a space-efficient row-based binary log format for UPDATE statements that modify a JSON value using only the functions JSON_SET, JSON_REPLACE, and JSON_REMOVE. For such updates, only the modified parts of the JSON document are included in the binary log, so small changes of big documents may need significantly less space.
This variable determines the size of the cache for the binary log to hold nontransactional statements issued during a transaction.
Maximum number of rows to keep in the writeset history.

Selects the source of dependency information from which to assess which transactions can be executed in parallel by the slave's multi-threaded applier. Possible values are COMMIT_ORDER, WRITESET and WRITESET_SESSION.
This variable controls the block encryption mode for block-based algorithms such as AES. It affects encryption for AES_ENCRYPT() and AES_DECRYPT().

Enable parallel execution on the slave of all uncommitted threads already in the prepare phase, without violating consistency.

When this option is enabled, the slave examines checksums read from the relay log, in the event of a mismatch, the slave stops with an error.

If a replication slave SQL thread fails to execute a transaction because of an InnoDB deadlock or because the transaction's execution time exceeded InnoDB's innodb_lock_wait_timeout, it automatically retries slave_transaction_retries times before stopping with an error.

If the value of this variable is greater than 0, the MySQL server synchronizes its relay log to disk (using fdatasync()) after every sync_relay_log writes to the relay log.
If the value of this variable is greater than 0, a replication slave synchronizes its relay-log.info file to disk (using fdatasync()) after every sync_relay_log_info transactions.
Causes SYSYDATE() to be an alias for NOW(). Replication related

Number of threads to be cached. Doesn't improve perf for good thread implementations.

The protocols permitted by the server for encrypted connections.

There is a per-transaction memory pool from which various transaction-related allocations take memory. For every allocation that cannot be satisfied from the pool because it has insufficient memory available, the pool is incremented.
Reserved for future use.

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall continuously monitor and control the security and functioning of ICT systems and tools and minimise the impact of ICT risk by deploying appropriate ICT security tools, policies and procedures. finding_info.title: DORA-Art28

checks.desc:
**Amazon S3 bucket policies** are analyzed for statements that grant **cross-account access**.

Any policy that names principals outside the owning account (other account IDs or `Principal: "*"`) is treated as cross-account; absence of a policy implies no cross-account grants.

message: S3 Bucket ref3tier-dev-app-kyll6s does not have correct cross region replication configuration.
status_detail: S3 Bucket ref3tier-dev-app-kyll6s does not have correct cross region replication configuration.

requirement_attributes.article_title: Backup policies and procedures, restoration and recovery procedures and methods

finding_info.desc: Financial entities shall develop and document backup policies and procedures specifying the scope of data subject to backup and the minimum frequency of the backup, as well as restoration and recovery procedures and methods. Backups must be encrypted, retained, and resources must be designed for recoverability across availability zones and regions.
finding_info.title: DORA-Art12

checks.desc:
**Amazon S3 buckets** use **cross-Region replication** with `versioning` and an enabled rule that targets a destination bucket in a different AWS Region.

Buckets with same-Region targets, missing destinations, or disabled `versioning` don't meet this replication posture.
checks.name: S3 bucket has cross-region replication configured to a bucket in a different region

compliance.control: Financial entities shall develop and document backup policies and procedures specifying the scope of data subject to backup and the minimum frequency of the backup, as well as restoration and recovery procedures and methods.
